column1: flow
postcomment — association — post — label="postreference"; explanation="postcomment references a post"
businessuser — association — user — label="user"; explanation="businessuser has a user"
post — association — user — label="author"; explanation="post is authored by a user"
chatlist — association — user — label="me"; explanation="chatlist contains a user"
chatlist — association — user — label="chattedwith"; explanation="chatlist contains a list of users"
neighborhood — association — user — label="neighborhood"; explanation="user belongs to a neighborhood"
neighborhood — association — post — label="neighborhood"; explanation="post belongs to a neighborhood"
flow 1: user creates a postcomment, which is associated with a post through the postreference attribute.
flow 2: user creates a post, which is associated with a neighborhood through the neighborhood attribute.
flow 3: user creates a chatlist, which contains a user (me) and a list of users (chattedwith).

this diagram models a social networking application with classes for user, post, postcomment, businessuser, chatlist, and neighborhood. the main flows involve creating and associating instances of these classes, such as a user creating a post or a postcomment. key edge cases include the association between a post and a neighborhood, and the relationship between a user and their chatlist.

no -> usercreateprocedure — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="user not created"

no -> - none — type=transition; label="no"; explanation="proceed with failure"

the diagram models the user interaction flow starting from the home screen.
users can navigate to different sections by clicking specific icons or interacting with the map.
there is a loop back to the home screen when interacting with the menu and clicking the home access icon.
key edge cases include navigating away from the home screen to other sections and returning to the home screen.

flow 1: generaldashboard -> scorri posts condivisi presenti in bacheca -> cliccato specifico post -> attend carico commenti -> fullscreenpost -> aggiungi un commento -> leavecomment -> attend salvataggio
this activity diagram models the interaction process for posting and commenting in a general forum section.

the user navigates through the dashboard, selects a section, browses shared posts, clicks on a specific post, loads comments, views the full post, adds a comment, submits it, and waits for the comment to be saved.
there is a conditional branch when clicking on a specific post, leading to either loading comments or proceeding to the full post view.
the process includes waiting for various actions such as comment loading and saving.
the diagram captures the sequential steps involved in interacting with a post in a general forum section.

the diagram models the process of viewing profiles of residents, professionals, and business owners.
it includes steps for adding profiles to contacts, viewing unsold items, promotions, and making appointments.
there are conditional paths
main -> storeddatadb — type=message; label="valid credentials"; explanation="authorization"
main -> usercreateprocedure — type=message; label="not exist"; explanation="authentication"
usercreateprocedure -> storeddatadb — type=message; label="create new entry"; explanation="create new entry"
flow 1: main sends "valid credentials" to storeddatadb for authorization
flow 2: if storeddatadb does not exist, main sends "not exist" to usercreateprocedure for authentication
flow 3: usercreateprocedure creates a new entry in storeddatadb
this sequence diagram models the process of user existence and creation within a system.
the main flow involves the main component sending valid credentials to the storeddatadb for authorization.
if the user does not exist, the main component sends a "not exist" message to the usercreateprocedure for authentication.
the usercreateprocedure then creates a new entry in the storeddatadb.
the diagram highlights the interaction between the main, storeddatadb, and usercreateprocedure components, focusing on the authorization and authentication processes.
